---
title: "Workshop W2"
author: Dylan Hoang
format: 
  html: 
    theme: cerulean
  pdf: default
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

The `echo: false` will hide the output of the code chunk
    
`warning: false` will remove any warning in the output, but here it just does not show any output at all

`eval: false` will not run code
```{r}
#| warning: false

library(rmarkdown)
library(knitr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
```

## Introduction
"In this tutorial we are looking at *Avian Influenza* cases detected globally since 1997" 

Here is the link for the website: [US CDC](https://www.cdc.gov/bird-flu/php/avian-flu-summary/chart-epi-curve-ah5n1.html)

## Reading Avian Influenza Data

```{r Reading data}
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
dat <- read_csv("data/avian_influenza_numbers.csv")
```

```{r}
head(dat, 10)
```

```{r}
glimpse(dat)
```

```{r}
str(dat)
```
> * This dataset has `r ncol(dat)` number of columns and `r nrow(dat)` number of rows.

### Why is it important to know the dimension of your dataset?

It is important to have a grasp into your dataset, giving you more understanding with what you are dealing with

### What are the variable names in the dataset?

 [1] "Range"          "Month"          "Vietnam"        "Turkey"         "Thailand"       "Iraq"          
 [7] "Indonesia"      "Egypt"          "Djibouti"       "China"          "Cambodia"       "Nigeria"       
[13] "Azerbaijan"     "Pakistan"       "Myanmar"        "Laos"           "Bangladesh"     "Canada"        
[19] "India"          "Nepal"          "United Kingdom" "Spain"          "United States"  "Ecuador"       
[25] "Chile"          "Australia"     

- range: recorded time range year-year
- month: one month snapshot inside the range

# Exercise 4
1. 
```{r}
      
```
2. 
- *Month*: character
- *Australia*: number
- *Egypt*: number
- *United States*: number

> The type for "Month" is not correct, it should be in Datetime/Date


```{r}
#| tbl-cap: "Summary of number of cases of Avian Influenza"
kable(caption = "Summary" data_cleaned)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
data_cleaned |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Egypt)) +
  geom_histogram()

```

```{r}
data_cleaned |>
  pivot_longer(Australia:`United States`) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = name)) + 
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = 'dodge')   # This does the exact same thing as geom_col()
```



```{r}
data_cleaned |>
  pivot_longer(Australia:`United States`) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = Month, y = value, fill = name)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +  # This does the exact same thing as geom_col() + 
  facet_wrap(~name)
  labs(x = "Month", y = NULL)
```

```{r}

```
